result.markdown:
---
title: "Summarise"
---

<center>
![](images/dplyr_summarise.png){style="width:200px"}
</center>

The function `summarise()` allows you to get a summary of values based on the groups within a grouped __tibble__.
It will produce a __tibble__ of the summarised information.

Various helper functions can be used to get specific info including:

- `n()`: Count number of instances of group
- `mean()`: Calculate means of a columns
- `median()`: Calculate median of columns
- `sd()`: Calculate standard deviation
- `IQR()`: Calculate interquartile range
    - iqr = upper quartile - lower quartile
- `min(): Minimum value
- `max()`: Maximum value
- `first()`: Extract first value
- `last()`: Extract last value
- `nth()`: Extract specified nth value

[Tidyverse reference page](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org//reference/summarise.html)


::: {.cell}

:::


## Dataset

For demonstration we'll load the `amphibian_div_tbl` data from the [mgrtibbles package](/datasets/mgrtibbles.qmd) (hyperlink includes install instructions). 

We'll remove any rows with `NA`s as `NA`s cause mean and other other calculations to return `NA`.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
#Load package
library("mgrtibbles")
#amphibian_div_tbl tibble for demonstration
amphibian_div_tbl <- mgrtibbles::amphibian_div_tbl |> tidyr::drop_na()
#View tibble
amphibian_div_tbl
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 65 × 15
   Species                  IUCN.Red.List.Status iucn_2cat Order   Family  Genus
   <chr>                    <fct>                <fct>     <chr>   <chr>   <chr>
 1 Acris crepitans          Least Concern (LC)   LC        Anura   Hylidae Acris
 2 Acris gryllus            Least Concern (LC)   LC        Anura   Hylidae Acris
 3 Ambystoma jeffersonianum Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Ambyst… Amby…
 4 Ambystoma macrodactylum  Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Ambyst… Amby…
 5 Ambystoma maculatum      Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Ambyst… Amby…
 6 Ambystoma texanum        Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Ambyst… Amby…
 7 Ambystoma tigrinum       Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Ambyst… Amby…
 8 Amphiuma means           Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Amphiu… Amph…
 9 Amphiuma tridactylum     Least Concern (LC)   LC        Caudata Amphiu… Amph…
10 Anaxyrus boreas          Least Concern (LC)   LC        Anura   Bufoni… Anax…
# ℹ 55 more rows
# ℹ 9 more variables: Age_at_maturity_min_y <dbl>, Age_at_maturity_max_y <dbl>,
#   Body_size_mm <dbl>, Longevity_max_y <dbl>, Litter_size_min_n <dbl>,
#   Litter_size_max_n <dbl>, Offspring_size_min_mm <dbl>,
#   Offspring_size_max_mm <fct>, Development <chr>
```


:::
:::


## Summarise

The default of `summarise()` is to produce a __tibble__ of unique group values.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |> 
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    dplyr::summarise()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 1
  IUCN.Red.List.Status            
  <fct>                           
1 Least Concern (LC)              
2 Near Threatened (NT)            
3 Vulnerable (VU)                 
4 Endangered (EN)                 
5 Least Concern (LC) - Provisional
```


:::
:::


## Count

The counts of each unique value can be added with `n()`. 
This is the same as the [`count()`](/dplyr/count.qmd) function.

Notice that the new column's name is specified before the `=` sign. 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |> 
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    dplyr::summarise(n = n())
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 2
  IUCN.Red.List.Status                 n
  <fct>                            <int>
1 Least Concern (LC)                  55
2 Near Threatened (NT)                 5
3 Vulnerable (VU)                      3
4 Endangered (EN)                      1
5 Least Concern (LC) - Provisional     1
```


:::
:::


## Min and max

The `min()` and `max()` functions can be used to extract the minimum and maximum values from specified columns/variables for each group.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |>
    #Group by IUCN.Red.List.Status
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    #Summarise
    dplyr::summarise(
                    min_body_size = min(Body_size_mm), 
                    max_body_size = max(Body_size_mm))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 3
  IUCN.Red.List.Status             min_body_size max_body_size
  <fct>                                    <dbl>         <dbl>
1 Least Concern (LC)                          33          1162
2 Near Threatened (NT)                        51           171
3 Vulnerable (VU)                             51           178
4 Endangered (EN)                             87            87
5 Least Concern (LC) - Provisional            48            48
```


:::
:::


## Mean and median

The `mean()` function can be used to calculate the mean of groups and specified columns/variables.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |>
    #Group by IUCN.Red.List.Status
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    #Summarise
    dplyr::summarise(n = n(), 
                    mean_body_size = mean(Body_size_mm), 
                    mean_max_litter_size = mean(Litter_size_max_n)                    )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 4
  IUCN.Red.List.Status                 n mean_body_size mean_max_litter_size
  <fct>                            <int>          <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Least Concern (LC)                  55           173.                2570.
2 Near Threatened (NT)                 5           114.                 369.
3 Vulnerable (VU)                      3           133                   19 
4 Endangered (EN)                      1            87                  350 
5 Least Concern (LC) - Provisional     1            48                 1012 
```


:::
:::


The `median()` function calculates medians.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |>
    #Group by IUCN.Red.List.Status
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    #Summarise
    dplyr::summarise(n = n(), 
                    mean_body_size = mean(Body_size_mm),
                    median_body_size = median(Body_size_mm))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 4
  IUCN.Red.List.Status                 n mean_body_size median_body_size
  <fct>                            <int>          <dbl>            <dbl>
1 Least Concern (LC)                  55           173.              127
2 Near Threatened (NT)                 5           114.              111
3 Vulnerable (VU)                      3           133               170
4 Endangered (EN)                      1            87                87
5 Least Concern (LC) - Provisional     1            48                48
```


:::
:::


## Standard deviation and IQR

The `sd()` and `IQR()` function calculate standard deviation and inter quartile range (upper quartile - lower quartile).

__Note:__ `NA`s are provided for standard deviation and 0 for IQR if there is only one value in the group.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |>
    #Group by IUCN.Red.List.Status
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    #Summarise
    dplyr::summarise(n = n(),
                    sd_body_size = sd(Body_size_mm),
                    iqr_body_size = IQR(Body_size_mm),
                    )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 4
  IUCN.Red.List.Status                 n sd_body_size iqr_body_size
  <fct>                            <int>        <dbl>         <dbl>
1 Least Concern (LC)                  55        200.           94.5
2 Near Threatened (NT)                 5         51.7          83  
3 Vulnerable (VU)                      3         71.1          63.5
4 Endangered (EN)                      1         NA             0  
5 Least Concern (LC) - Provisional     1         NA             0  
```


:::
:::


## First, last and nth values

The first, last, and nth values can be extracted with the functions: `first()`, `last()`, and `nth()`.

__Note:__ `NA`s are provided for as some of the `nth()` values as there is only one value in those groups. `first()` and `last()` work as the single value is the first and last value.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
amphibian_div_tbl |>
    #Group by IUCN.Red.List.Status
    dplyr::group_by(IUCN.Red.List.Status) |>
    #Summarise
    dplyr::summarise(n = n(),
                    first_body_size = first(Body_size_mm),
                    last_body_size = last(Body_size_mm),
                    second_body_size = nth(Body_size_mm, 2)
                    )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 5
  IUCN.Red.List.Status         n first_body_size last_body_size second_body_size
  <fct>                    <int>           <dbl>          <dbl>            <dbl>
1 Least Concern (LC)          55              38            197               33
2 Near Threatened (NT)         5              51             76              111
3 Vulnerable (VU)              3              51            170              178
4 Endangered (EN)              1              87             87               NA
5 Least Concern (LC) - Pr…     1              48             48               NA
```


:::
:::
